name: slide-mcp-server
description: Ask Claude about your Slide backups: find files, restore data, boot recovery VMs, triage alerts. Designed for MSP technicians; no IDs to memorise.
long_description:
Slide MCP Server v5.1.0 connects Claude Desktop directly to your Slide BCDR / backup environment with a reliable, novice-first toolkit.

Claude reaches for this extension whenever you mention Slide, BCDR, business continuity, disaster recovery, backups, snapshots, restores, recovery VMs, file recovery, image exports, DR networks, audit logs, or unresolved alerts. You never have to spell out 'use the Slide MCP'.

What you can ask in plain English:
  * 'Are all my Slide boxes healthy?'
  * 'Did backups run last night for ACME?'
  * 'Find Q4-budget.xlsx on Bob's laptop and restore Tuesday's version.'
  * 'Boot a recovery VM for DC-01.'
  * 'What unresolved alerts do I have? Sort worst first.'
  * 'What changed in the last 24 hours?'

No IDs to memorise: every tool that needs an agent_id / device_id / client_id also accepts name_hint - pass a hostname, display name, or any substring ('Bob's laptop', 'ACME', 'DC-01') and the server fuzzy-resolves it server-side. Ambiguous matches return a structured list of candidates so the assistant can ask you to pick one.

New in v5.1.0:
  * Faster complete inventory and health through three concurrent, fully paginated bulk reads.
  * Bounded API requests with hard deadlines, response-size limits, capped backoff, and mutation-safe retry rules.
  * Runtime JSON Schema validation and compatibility coverage for Claude Desktop and OpenAI Codex protocol profiles.
  * A real offline contract/subprocess harness plus live read-only smoke and cross-platform CI.
  * Correct Linux amd64/arm64 selection inside the MCPB and legacy installer download aliases.
  * Developer ID signed and Apple-notarized universal macOS delivery, enforced before release.

Also includes slide_help discovery, 6 slash-command prompts, 11 slide:// resources, server-side fuzzy name resolution, --doctor / --debug diagnostics, next_steps hints, and three permission tiers (read-only / safe / full).

This bundle includes signed and notarized binaries for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (amd64 + arm64), and Windows (amd64); the right binary is launched automatically for your machine.

Generate an API token at console.slide.tech under My Settings -> API Tokens. The token is stored by Claude Desktop and only sent to the slide-mcp-server process; it is never written into any project file.

tools.name: slide_help
tools.description: Discover Slide backup, BCDR, restore, recovery, alert, and troubleshooting capabilities.

tools.name: slide_overview
tools.description: View Slide client, device, and agent inventory and health.

tools.name: slide_files
tools.description: Search, browse, restore, and push files from Slide snapshots.

tools.name: slide_recovery
tools.description: Boot recovery VMs, export images, and manage DR networks.

tools.name: slide_audit
tools.description: Query the Slide account audit log.

tools.name: slide_admin
tools.description: Manage Slide users and account settings.

tools.name: slide_devices
tools.description: Inspect and manage Slide backup appliances.

tools.name: slide_agents
tools.description: Inspect and manage agents on protected systems.

tools.name: slide_snapshots
tools.description: Inspect Slide backup snapshots and verification results.

tools.name: slide_backups
tools.description: Inspect backup status and start backups.

api_key.title: Slide API Token (looks like tk_xxxxxxxxxxxx_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx)
api_key.description: Paste your Slide API token. Generate one at console.slide.tech -> My Settings -> API Tokens. The token gives full access to your Slide account; it is stored by Claude Desktop and only forwarded to the slide-mcp-server process at launch. To verify a token outside Claude Desktop, run: slide-mcp-server --api-key <token> --doctor

tools_mode.title: Tool permissions (type one of: read-only, safe, full)
tools_mode.description: read-only = only list/get/search/browse (good for monitoring dashboards). safe (default, recommended) = everything except deletes and device poweroff/reboot (covers restores, backup launch, agent/device settings, alert resolution). full = unrestricted, including deletes and device power-cycle. Leave blank for the safe default. Legacy aliases reporting/restores/full-safe still work.
